mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth reflects real output expansion; sustained increases signal corporate revenue upside, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly greater than zero over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income and consumer demand compression, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently high federal funds rates and declining industrial production generate a joint drag on corporate discount rates and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that is stronger than either factor alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and INDPRO does not significantly improve prediction accuracy (F-test p > 0.05) over single-factor models in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 5.5 percent, a quarter-over-quarter slowdown in real GDP growth signals an overheating economy vulnerable to monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is absent in high-unemployment regimes.
failure_condition: In walk-forward data, GDPC1 slowdown does not produce negative 60-day returns when UNRATE is below 5.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal is equally strong when UNRATE is above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4 percent raises short-term financing costs and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Nominal retail sales growth exceeding contemporaneous CPI inflation indicates expanding real consumer demand, and the joint combination of rising nominal sales with contained inflation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fails to outperform a univariate RSAFS model in predicting 20-day forward returns (Diebold-Mariano test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals enduring labor market strength and wage-driven consumer spending power, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth does not coincide with average positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A trailing three-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals acute factory-sector contraction and inventory destocking, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when INDPRO has declined >1% over the prior 3 months.
